name: Tagged
comment:
A module that allows you to "tag" a value.

@docs Tagged, tag, retag, untag, map, ap, bind, extend

unions.name: Tagged
unions.comment:
A Type that pairs a `value` with a `tag`.

The `tag` is ignored at runtime as evidenced by the only value constructor:

```elm
Tagged : value -> Tagged tag value
```

values.name: ap
values.comment:
Useful for composing functions together infix:

```elm
foo =
  Array.set `map` index `ap` value `ap` arr
```
values.type: Tagged tag (oldValue -> newValue) -> Tagged tag oldValue -> Tagged tag newValue

values.name: bind
values.comment: Useful for restricting the tag created in a polymorphic function.
values.type: (oldValue -> Tagged tag newValue) -> Tagged tag oldValue -> Tagged tag newValue

values.name: extend
values.comment:
Useful when you have a function that throws away a tag prematurely,
but you still need the tag later.
values.type: (Tagged tag oldValue -> newValue) -> Tagged tag oldValue -> Tagged tag newValue

values.name: map
values.comment:
Useful for composing functions together infix:

```elm
foo =
  Array.set `map` index `ap` value `ap` arr
```
values.type: (oldValue -> newValue) -> Tagged tag oldValue -> Tagged tag newValue

values.name: retag
values.comment:
Explicitly changes the tag of a value.

Forces you to recognize that the value is being interpreted differently from before.
values.type: Tagged oldTag value -> Tagged newTag value

values.name: tag
values.comment: An alias for the `Tagged` value constructor.

values.name: untag
values.comment: We can remove the tag when we're done making additional compile-time assertions.
